findings: Backward packet length features show high sensitivity to class-conditioned structural variation, indicated by variance ratios > 15 and skewed unique value counts between BENIGN and DDoS classes.
Dependency profiles of these features are concentrated within a narrow structural cluster, reinforcing their representation-sensitive nature under class imbalance.
No local contradictions or instability signals were detected in the dependency contextualization steps.
limitations: Analysis limited to local evidence within backward packet length features; cross-feature or global dataset effects remain unassessed.
